subject: ap-chemistry
title: Molecular Structure & Bonding
stimulus:
A student learns that ionic compounds have significant covalent character when a cation has a polarizing effect
on a large anion. As a result, the student hypothesizes that salts composed of small cations and large anions
should have relatively low melting points.

parts.prompt: Select two compounds from the table and explain how the data support the student’s hypothesis.

scoring_points.description: correct selection of two compounds with explanation of data support

required_elements: Valid justification based on chemical principles
alternatives.correct_example: Select two compounds from the table that support the hypothesis about molecular structure and properties. Compare their boiling points or other data to the predicted trend based on molecular size/polarity.

common_traps: Stating answer without connecting to the underlying chemical principle

parts.prompt:
Identify a compound from the table that can be dissolved in water to produce a basic solution. Write the net
ionic equation for the reaction that occurs to cause the solution to be basic.

scoring_points.description: correct compound identification and net ionic equation for basic solution

alternatives.correct_example: A compound such as Na2CO3 or NaHCO3 produces a basic solution. Net ionic equation: CO32−(aq) + H2O(l) ⇌ HCO3−(aq) + OH−(aq)
